dsl_rule.translation_notes: Extreme condition modeled as price_pos_20 < 0.05 (below 5th percentile) or > 0.95 (above 95th percentile) equivalent to 2+ std devs from 20-period MA. Reversal pattern approximated using wick ratios (hammer/pin bar = lower_wick > body; engulfing = upper_wick > body). Volume confirmation via vol_ratio_20. Exit at mean via sma_20 crossover or RSI mean-reversion threshold. Stop loss set to 0.8 ATR as decimal equivalent (0.8% of entry price as proxy for 0.8x ATR multiple).

backtest_rule.reason: Extreme condition modeled as price_pos_20 < 0.05 (below 5th percentile) or > 0.95 (above 95th percentile) equivalent to 2+ std devs from 20-period MA. Reversal pattern approximated using wick ratios (hammer/pin bar = lower_wick > body; engulfing = upper_wick > body). Volume confirmation via vol_ratio_20. Exit at mean via sma_20 crossover or RSI mean-reversion threshold. Stop loss set to 0.8 ATR as decimal equivalent (0.8% of entry price as proxy for 0.8x ATR multiple).

source_rule_text: A trader should adopt a mean-reversion or turning-point strategy with tight stops rather than a trend-following strategy with wide stops, because markets trend only ~15% of the time and the best risk-adjusted returns come from capturing reversals at extremes.
